name: Pennsylvania Pharmacy Technician Registration
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description:
Pennsylvania Board of Pharmacy
P.O. Box 2649
Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Pennsylvania pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Pennsylvania State Board of Pharmacy snapshot lists a $30 initial pharmacy technician registration fee and a $70 biennial renewal fee.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Pennsylvania?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pennsylvania does not publish a pharmacy technician processing timeline on its public registration snapshot page. Check the board portal for status updates.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Pennsylvania Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Pennsylvania.
